claimText: The National Air Quality Index (AQI) classifies air quality into exactly six categories ranging from Good to Severe.
evidenceExcerpt: AQI categorizes air quality into six ranges: Good (0-50), Satisfactory (51-100), Moderate (101-200), Poor (201-300), Very Poor (300-400), and Severe (>400).
examUse: Statement-based question testing the number of AQI categories and the highest severity level.

claimText: The National Air Quality Index (AQI) monitors exactly eight pollutants: PM10, PM2.5, NO2, SO2, CO, O3, NH3, and Pb.
evidenceExcerpt: Monitored Pollutants: PM10, PM2.5, NO2, SO2, CO, O3, NH3, Pb.
examUse: Multiple-choice question asking to identify which greenhouse gases or pollutants are NOT part of the AQI.
